name: Peak Peñabón – trail running circular from Trevélez (SL-A 78)
name_original: Pico Peñabón – trail running circular desde Trevélez (SL-A 78)
region_intro: Hiking around Trevélez offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra Nevada National Park, including towering peaks like Mulhacén and Alcazaba. The region is characterized by deep valleys, steep gorges carved by the Trevélez River, and glacial formations such as Siete Lagunas. Trails wind through diverse ecosystems, from forests of holm oaks to alpine pastures and high-altitude terrain. Best hiking trails around Trevélez The most popular hiking route is Pico Peñabón – circular from Trevélez (SL-A 78), a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of Trevélez and the surrounding peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parque Nacional Sierra Nevada – circular to Siete Lagunas from Trevélez, a difficult 13.3 miles (21.5 km) path. This strenuous ascent leads to a spectacular glacial valley with alpine pastures. Local hikers also love the Trevélez ham – Hondera Lagoon loop from Trevélez, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through varied mountain terrain, often completed in about 8 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Trevélez is defined by majestic mountain peaks, glacial valleys, and lush river valleys. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging multi-day treks. The routes in Trevélez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Trevélez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Trevélez?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes in the Trevélez area, offering a wide range of experiences. You'll find options from easy strolls to challenging multi-day treks through the Sierra Nevada National Park.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Trevélez?
A: Yes, Trevélez offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31571688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trevélez village – Trevélez River loop from Barrio Alto&lt;/a&gt;, a pleasant 5 km walk that takes about 1 hour 32 minutes, following the picturesque Trevélez River.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Trevélez?
A: Hiking in Trevélez immerses you in the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra Nevada. You'll encounter towering peaks like Mulhacén, deep valleys and gorges carved by the Trevélez River, and glacial formations such as the spectacular Siete Lagunas. Trails wind through diverse ecosystems, from holm oak forests to alpine pastures and high-altitude terrain, often adorned with wildflowers in spring.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting from Trevélez?
A: Yes, many routes in Trevélez are circular, allowing you to start and end in the village. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980634599/pico-penabon-circular-from-trevelez-sl-a-78" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñabón – circular from Trevélez (SL-A 78)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980546869/parque-nacional-sierra-nevada-circular-to-siete-lagunas-from-trevelez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional Sierra Nevada – circular to Siete Lagunas from Trevélez&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging full-day loop to a stunning glacial valley.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the stunning natural scenery, hikers can discover several points of interest. The challenging routes often lead to significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/111802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mulhacén Summit&lt;/a&gt; or offer views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Veleta viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/420302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Virgin of the Snows&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Trevélez?
A: The best times to visit Trevélez for hiking are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant, with wildflowers blooming in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter brings challenging conditions and requires alpine experience for higher peaks.

Q: Are there any hikes that follow the Trevélez River?
A: Yes, the Trevélez River carves through the landscape, creating lush valleys, and several trails follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10305049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trevélez ham – Trevélez River loop from Trevélez&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.7 km route that offers picturesque views with abundant greenery and the sound of running water.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Trevélez?
A: The hiking routes in Trevélez are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from nearly 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic mountain scenery, the diverse terrain, and the sense of adventure found on trails leading to high-altitude lakes and peaks.

Q: Are there any challenging multi-day treks available from Trevélez?
A: Trevélez serves as an excellent base for challenging multi-day treks, particularly for those aiming to summit the highest peaks of the Sierra Nevada, such as Mulhacén and Alcazaba. The hike to Siete Lagunas is a strenuous ascent often used as a camping spot for these longer expeditions, requiring good fitness and alpine experience.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic views of Trevélez and the surrounding mountains?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980634599/pico-penabon-circular-from-trevelez-sl-a-78" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñabón – circular from Trevélez (SL-A 78)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its spectacular panoramic views of Trevélez itself, the valley, and the imposing south faces of Mulhacén and Alcazaba.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or waterfalls along the hiking routes?
A: While specific waterfalls are not highlighted, the Trevélez River, originating from melting snow, creates lush valleys. The glacial valley of Siete Lagunas features several small lakes, and the region's rivers and lagoons offer opportunities for refreshing dips, especially during warmer months, though always exercise caution and respect local regulations.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Trevélez?
A: The Sierra Nevada National Park is rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot various bird species, mountain goats (ibex), and unique flora, especially in alpine areas like Siete Lagunas, where endemic plants such as the 'snow star' can be found.
tips: One of the classic ascents from Trevélez, a town over which this mass looms almost threateningly. The Local Path of Andalusia nº78 reaches it, reconditioned and signposted in 2010.

This town, located at an altitude of 1,476 m on the southwestern slopes of Mulhacén, is not exactly the highest town in Spain as some claim, but its municipal area is, since the Mulhacén peak is located there. It is for this reason that some of its agricultural lands are also among the highest in Europe. Its name comes from the Latin "velex" which means 'valley'.
